paper_title: Ice Core Data Collection and Description

methods.description: Process for extracting and preparing ice core samples for isotope analysis using the West Antarctic Ice Sheet Divide core (WDC) to measure the ratios of 2H/1H and 18O/16O, which are proxies for local temperature and regional atmospheric circulation.

steps.description: The record was analyzed using a Picarro Inc. cavity ring-down spectroscopy (CRDS) instrument, coupled to a continuous flow analysis (CFA) system.
steps.searchable_summary: Use CRDS-CFA system to analyze ice core samples for isotope ratios

steps.description: The data are reported in delta (δ) notation relative to the baseline Vienna Standard Mean Ocean Water (VSMOW) and normalized to the Standard Light Antarctic Water (SLAP) scale.
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize isotope data to VSMOW and SLAP scales

steps.description: The δ values were determined by δ = 1000(Rsample/RVSMOW −1), where R is the isotopic ratio 18O/16O or D/H.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate delta values for isotope ratios

steps.description: Water-isotope traces measured on the Picarro instrument were recorded at a rate of 1.18 Hz (0.85 s intervals).
Ice samples were moved through the CRDS-CFA system at a rate of 2.5 cm/min, yielding millimeter resolution. The data were then averaged over non-overlapping 0.5 cm bins.
steps.searchable_summary: Average isotope data over 0.5 cm bins

steps.description: For each of these data points, an age was determined using the WDC depth-age scale, providing climate data from 0–68 ka.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine age for each data point using WDC depth-age scale
